event: Coronation of the Virgin in Heaven
symbols: rainbow encircling the throne — God's covenant faithfulness and heavenly glory
concentric circles of light — the hierarchy of heavenly glory radiating from the divine center
dove at center — the Holy Spirit presiding over the coronation
vast assembly of saints — the communion of saints and the Church Triumphant
golden luminous atmosphere — the uncreated light of divine presence
central throne — the seat of divine authority from which the coronation proceeds
description: A vast celestial scene rendered in warm golden-brown tones showing an enormous gathering of figures arranged in concentric semicircles around a central luminous point. At the center, a radiant figure is enthroned beneath a prominent rainbow of concentric colored arcs — red, blue, and green. A dove-like form descends from the apex. Hundreds of figures in white robes fill the surrounding space, arranged in tiers on clouds, creating the impression of a heavenly amphitheater. The entire composition glows with golden light emanating from the center outward.
theological_description: This sweeping vision of the Coronation of the Virgin draws on the iconographic tradition of Revelation's heavenly throne room, where the entire company of heaven gathers in worship. The rainbow surrounding the central throne directly evokes Revelation 4:3: 'A rainbow that shone like an emerald encircled the throne.' The vast assembly of white-robed figures recalls the 'great multitude that no one could count, from every nation' (Revelation 7:9) standing before the throne. The dove descending from above represents the Holy Spirit, whose presence sanctifies the coronation. The concentric arrangement of the heavenly court reflects the medieval theological understanding of celestial hierarchy — the closer to the center, the closer to God. The Virgin's coronation, while not narrated in Scripture, draws on the imagery of the queen at the king's right hand in Psalm 45:9 and the woman crowned with twelve stars in Revelation 12:1. The painting presents the fullness of the Church Triumphant — all the redeemed gathered in the glory that is both their reward and their worship, with Mary honored as the first and greatest among the saints.

scripture_refs.reason: The throne in heaven surrounded by a rainbow — the direct visual source for the central throne and rainbow imagery
The great multitude in white robes before the throne — the vast assembly of saints depicted
The queen stands at the king's right hand in gold — traditional typology for the Coronation of the Virgin
The woman clothed with the sun and crowned with stars — the Marian eschatological figure
The heavenly Jerusalem with thousands of angels and the spirits of the righteous made perfect
